task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Amateur' module tab or window is no longer visible in the main GPredict interface.

task_completion.description: Add a ground station named 'Miami_NHC' with specific coordinates
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Miami_NHC' in the ground station list, module configuration dialogs, or module headers. If the Edit/Add Ground Station dialog is visible in any screenshot, verify Latitude is 25.7543, Longitude is 80.3823 W (or -80.3823), and Altitude is 2m.

task_completion.description: Create a module named 'GEO_WX' with 'Map' layout and specific GEO satellites
task_completion.visual_evidence: A module tab or window named 'GEO_WX' is visible, displaying a 'Map' visual layout. The module must show the satellites GOES 16, GOES 18, and METEOSAT 11.

task_completion.description: Create a module named 'LEO_WX' with 'Polar' layout and specific LEO satellites
task_completion.visual_evidence: A module tab or window named 'LEO_WX' is visible, displaying a 'Polar' visual layout (radar-like circular view). The module must show the satellites NOAA 19, METOP-C, and SUOMI NPP.

task_completion.description: Configure GPredict to display UTC time
task_completion.visual_evidence: The time display in the GPredict interface (typically in the module headers or main window) explicitly shows 'UTC' instead of local time.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the GPredict graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through GPredict menus, dialog boxes, and module settings rather than just editing configuration files in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual GPredict application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application shown is the genuine GPredict satellite tracking software, not a text editor, web browser, or mock-up.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply type the expected answers into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The results are reflected in the actual GPredict interface, not just written out in a terminal or text document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GEO satellites NORAD IDs: GOES 16 (41866), GOES 18 (51850), METEOSAT 11 (40732). LEO satellites NORAD IDs: NOAA 19 (33591), METOP-C (43689), SUOMI NPP (37849). Miami NHC coordinates: Latitude 25.7543 N, Longitude 80.3823 W (-80.3823). Altitude must be set to 2 meters as requested by the task, despite the actual physical elevation of the building.
